answer:
BigCommerce APIs primarily use the X-Auth-Token header to authenticate requests with OAuth access tokens [1][2]. For REST and GraphQL Admin APIs, include the access token in your request header:

```http
GET https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{{STORE_HASH}}/v3/catalog/products
X-Auth-Token: {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}
```

For GraphQL Storefront APIs, use the Authorization header with a Bearer token:

```http
Authorization: Bearer {{TOKEN}}
```

Other specialized methods exist, such as using an Authorization header with a Bearer prefix for storefront requests [3] or utilizing an X-Store-Hash header alongside X-Auth-Token for B2B server-to-server requests [4].

results.title: API Accounts & OAuth Scopes | BigCommerce Developer Center
